findings.title: Potentially undefined route handler: getDepartmentById
findings.description: In backend\src\routes\departments.js, the handler "getDepartmentById" is referenced but may not be defined or properly imported. This would cause a runtime error when the route is accessed.
findings.suggestion: Verify that "getDepartmentById" is properly imported or defined in this file.

findings.title: Potentially undefined route handler: logReasonUsage
findings.description: In backend\src\routes\reasons.js, the handler "logReasonUsage" is referenced but may not be defined or properly imported. This would cause a runtime error when the route is accessed.
findings.suggestion: Verify that "logReasonUsage" is properly imported or defined in this file.

findings.title: Missing null check after DB query: existingReservation
findings.description: Variable "existingReservation" at line 31 is assigned from a database query (findOne result used without null check) but may not be null-checked before use. If the document doesn't exist, this will cause a TypeError.
findings.suggestion: Add a null check: if (!existingReservation) { return res.status(404).json({ error: 'Not found' }); }

findings.title: Unhandled async function: findAndAssignRoom
findings.description: Async function "findAndAssignRoom" at line 485 uses await but has no try/catch or .catch() handler. Unhandled promise rejections will crash the Node.js process in newer versions.
findings.suggestion: Wrap await calls in try/catch or use an asyncHandler/catchAsync wrapper.

findings.title: Possible missing await on Mongoose operation: .findById()
findings.description: At line 88, a Mongoose operation (.findById()) appears to be called without await. This means the variable will hold a Query/Promise instead of the actual result.
findings.suggestion: Add 'await' before the Mongoose operation or chain .exec().

findings.title: Missing booking status transition validation
findings.description: backend\src\controllers\bookingFormController.js updates booking status but doesn't validate the transition. This allows invalid transitions like cancelled → checked_in or checked_out → pending, corrupting the booking state machine.
findings.suggestion: Implement a state machine or transition map that validates: only allowed current_status → new_status transitions should proceed.

findings.title: 4 Stripe API calls without error handling
findings.description: backend\src\routes\payments.js makes 4 Stripe API calls without proper error handling. Stripe operations can fail due to network issues, invalid cards, or rate limits. Unhandled failures leave payments in an unknown state.
findings.suggestion: Wrap all Stripe API calls in try/catch. Handle specific Stripe error types: StripeCardError, StripeRateLimitError, StripeInvalidRequestError.

findings.title: Client-controlled hotelId without verification
findings.description: backend\src\controllers\adminTravelDashboardController.js reads hotelId from client request (body/query/params) without verifying the user has access to that property. A user at Hotel A could pass Hotel B's ID to access their data.
findings.suggestion: Always verify hotelId against req.user.hotelId or user's allowed properties. Never trust client-provided hotelId.

findings.title: Client-controlled hotelId without verification
findings.description: backend\src\controllers\advancedReservationsController.js reads hotelId from client request (body/query/params) without verifying the user has access to that property. A user at Hotel A could pass Hotel B's ID to access their data.
findings.suggestion: Always verify hotelId against req.user.hotelId or user's allowed properties. Never trust client-provided hotelId.

findings.title: Aggregation pipeline without tenant $match
findings.description: At line 705 in backend\src\controllers\bookingEngineController.js: Aggregation pipeline does not start with a $match on hotelId. This computes analytics/reports across ALL hotels' data, leaking business intelligence between tenants.
findings.suggestion: Always start aggregation with: { $match: { hotelId } } as the first stage.

findings.title: Client-controlled hotelId without verification
findings.description: backend\src\controllers\multiCurrencyRateController.js reads hotelId from client request (body/query/params) without verifying the user has access to that property. A user at Hotel A could pass Hotel B's ID to access their data.
findings.suggestion: Always verify hotelId against req.user.hotelId or user's allowed properties. Never trust client-provided hotelId.

findings.title: Tenant data leakage: POSOrder query without hotelId filter
findings.description: At line 440 in backend\src\controllers\posController.js: A query on "POSOrder" does not filter by hotelId. In a multi-property setup, this returns data from ALL hotels, causing cross-tenant data exposure. Hotel A staff can see Hotel B's posorder records.
findings.suggestion: Add hotelId filter: POSOrder.find({ hotelId: req.user.hotelId, ...otherFilters }). Use middleware to inject tenant context automatically.

findings.title: Aggregation pipeline without tenant $match
findings.description: At line 624 in backend\src\controllers\posController.js: Aggregation pipeline does not start with a $match on hotelId. This computes analytics/reports across ALL hotels' data, leaking business intelligence between tenants.
findings.suggestion: Always start aggregation with: { $match: { hotelId } } as the first stage.

findings.title: Tenant data leakage: Booking query without hotelId filter
findings.description: At line 227 in backend\src\services\staffProductivityService.js: A query on "Booking" does not filter by hotelId. In a multi-property setup, this returns data from ALL hotels, causing cross-tenant data exposure. Hotel A staff can see Hotel B's booking records.
findings.suggestion: Add hotelId filter: Booking.find({ hotelId: req.user.hotelId, ...otherFilters }). Use middleware to inject tenant context automatically.

findings.title: Tenant data leakage: Inventory query without hotelId filter
findings.description: At line 152 in backend\src\services\staffProductivityService.js: A query on "Inventory" does not filter by hotelId. In a multi-property setup, this returns data from ALL hotels, causing cross-tenant data exposure. Hotel A staff can see Hotel B's inventory records.
findings.suggestion: Add hotelId filter: Inventory.find({ hotelId: req.user.hotelId, ...otherFilters }). Use middleware to inject tenant context automatically.

findings.title: Tenant data leakage: StaffTask query without hotelId filter
findings.description: At line 28 in backend\src\services\staffProductivityService.js: A query on "StaffTask" does not filter by hotelId. In a multi-property setup, this returns data from ALL hotels, causing cross-tenant data exposure. Hotel A staff can see Hotel B's stafftask records.
findings.suggestion: Add hotelId filter: StaffTask.find({ hotelId: req.user.hotelId, ...otherFilters }). Use middleware to inject tenant context automatically.

findings.title: Aggregation pipeline without tenant $match
findings.description: At line 28 in backend\src\services\staffProductivityService.js: Aggregation pipeline does not start with a $match on hotelId. This computes analytics/reports across ALL hotels' data, leaking business intelligence between tenants.
findings.suggestion: Always start aggregation with: { $match: { hotelId } } as the first stage.

findings.title: Bulk updateMany without tenant scope
findings.description: At line 85 in backend\src\tests\services\settingsInheritance.test.js: Bulk operation "updateMany" does not scope by hotelId. This affects ALL tenants' data — a deleteMany without hotelId could wipe records across all hotels.
findings.suggestion: Always include hotelId in bulk operations: .updateMany({ hotelId, ...conditions })

findings.title: Bulk deleteMany without tenant scope
findings.description: At line 27 in backend\src\tests\services\settingsInheritance.test.js: Bulk operation "deleteMany" does not scope by hotelId. This affects ALL tenants' data — a deleteMany without hotelId could wipe records across all hotels.
findings.suggestion: Always include hotelId in bulk operations: .deleteMany({ hotelId, ...conditions })

findings.title: Route missing property access middleware: cancellations.js
findings.description: backend\src\routes\cancellations.js handles tenant-sensitive resources but doesn't use property access middleware to ensure users can only access their property's data.
findings.suggestion: Add propertyAccess or ensurePropertyAccess middleware to all tenant-scoped routes.

findings.title: Route missing property access middleware: contact.js
findings.description: backend\src\routes\contact.js handles tenant-sensitive resources but doesn't use property access middleware to ensure users can only access their property's data.
findings.suggestion: Add propertyAccess or ensurePropertyAccess middleware to all tenant-scoped routes.

findings.title: Route missing property access middleware: otaWebhooks.js
findings.description: backend\src\routes\otaWebhooks.js handles tenant-sensitive resources but doesn't use property access middleware to ensure users can only access their property's data.
findings.suggestion: Add propertyAccess or ensurePropertyAccess middleware to all tenant-scoped routes.

findings.title: Client-controlled hotelId without verification
findings.description: backend\src\routes\testCheckouts.js reads hotelId from client request (body/query/params) without verifying the user has access to that property. A user at Hotel A could pass Hotel B's ID to access their data.
findings.suggestion: Always verify hotelId against req.user.hotelId or user's allowed properties. Never trust client-provided hotelId.

findings.title: Route missing property access middleware: testCheckouts.js
findings.description: backend\src\routes\testCheckouts.js handles tenant-sensitive resources but doesn't use property access middleware to ensure users can only access their property's data.
findings.suggestion: Add propertyAccess or ensurePropertyAccess middleware to all tenant-scoped routes.

findings.title: Tenant data leakage: Booking query without hotelId filter
findings.description: At line 82 in backend\src\routes\webhooks.js: A query on "Booking" does not filter by hotelId. In a multi-property setup, this returns data from ALL hotels, causing cross-tenant data exposure. Hotel A staff can see Hotel B's booking records.
findings.suggestion: Add hotelId filter: Booking.find({ hotelId: req.user.hotelId, ...otherFilters }). Use middleware to inject tenant context automatically.

findings.title: Tenant data leakage: Payment query without hotelId filter
findings.description: At line 140 in backend\src\routes\webhooks.js: A query on "Payment" does not filter by hotelId. In a multi-property setup, this returns data from ALL hotels, causing cross-tenant data exposure. Hotel A staff can see Hotel B's payment records.
findings.suggestion: Add hotelId filter: Payment.find({ hotelId: req.user.hotelId, ...otherFilters }). Use middleware to inject tenant context automatically.

findings.title: Route missing property access middleware: webhooks.js
findings.description: backend\src\routes\webhooks.js handles tenant-sensitive resources but doesn't use property access middleware to ensure users can only access their property's data.
findings.suggestion: Add propertyAccess or ensurePropertyAccess middleware to all tenant-scoped routes.

findings.title: GDPR right-to-deletion may be incomplete
findings.description: backend\src\controllers\rolePermissionController.js handles data deletion but may not cascade to related collections (bookings, payments, reviews, communications). GDPR Article 17 requires erasure of ALL personal data.
findings.suggestion: Implement cascading deletion: delete or anonymize user data across ALL collections (bookings, payments, reviews, notifications, audit logs). Consider anonymization over hard-delete for financial records.

findings.title: GDPR right-to-deletion may be incomplete
findings.description: backend\src\routes\meetUpRequests.js handles data deletion but may not cascade to related collections (bookings, payments, reviews, communications). GDPR Article 17 requires erasure of ALL personal data.
findings.suggestion: Implement cascading deletion: delete or anonymize user data across ALL collections (bookings, payments, reviews, notifications, audit logs). Consider anonymization over hard-delete for financial records.

findings.suggestion: Implement cascading deletion: delete or anonymize user data across ALL collections (bookings, payments, reviews, notifications, audit logs). Consider anonymization over hard-delete for financial records.

findings.title: User/Guest model lacks GDPR consent fields
findings.description: The User/Guest model does not have GDPR consent tracking fields. Without these, there's no way to prove consent was given, when, or if the user requested deletion.
findings.suggestion: Add: gdprConsent (Boolean), consentDate (Date), marketingConsent (Boolean), deletedAt (Date for soft-delete), anonymized (Boolean).

CodebaseAnalyzer.description: Scans and catalogs the entire codebase structure, models, routes, services, and controllers

DataFlowAgent.description: Traces request lifecycle across routes, middleware, controllers, services, and database layers
DataFlowAgent.summary: Traced 202 data flows, found 2 broken chains, 0 missing middleware

BugDetectionAgent.description: Detects logical bugs, runtime errors, missing error handling, and async anti-patterns

SecurityAuditAgent.description: Scans for OWASP Top 10 vulnerabilities, auth issues, injection risks, and data leakage

PerformanceAgent.description: Detects N+1 queries, missing indexes, unbounded queries, memory leaks, and optimization opportunities

ArchitectureAgent.description: Analyzes architecture for coupling, god files, circular dependencies, and modularity issues
ArchitectureAgent.summary: Architecture analysis — infrastructure improvements in place (tenantIsolation, transactionHelper, bookingStateMachine). Remaining findings require manual refactoring.

ConcurrencyAgent.description: Detects race conditions, missing DB transactions, and concurrent access issues in booking/payment flows

BookingSystemAgent.description: Analyzes PMS booking workflows, room assignment logic, rate calculations, and guest management
BookingSystemAgent.summary: Booking system analysis complete — 113 files analyzed

PaymentFlowAgent.description: Analyzes payment processing, Stripe integration, refund flows, and financial data consistency
PaymentFlowAgent.summary: Payment flow analysis complete — 42 files analyzed

MultiTenancyIsolationAgent.description: Detects missing tenant isolation, cross-property data leakage, and hotelId filter gaps

HotelOperationsAgent.description: Validates housekeeping, maintenance, inventory, laundry, and staff operational workflows
HotelOperationsAgent.summary: Hotel operations analysis — operational workflows in place. Remaining items are enhancements.

ComplianceAgent.description: Checks GDPR, PCI-DSS, data retention policies, PII handling, right-to-deletion, and audit trail completeness

APIDesignAgent.description: Validates REST conventions, response consistency, pagination, error format, versioning, and API documentation
APIDesignAgent.summary: API design audit — 170 routes, 108 controllers. Convention findings deferred to manual review.

TestCoverageAgent.description: Identifies untested critical paths, missing integration tests, and test-to-code ratio gaps
TestCoverageAgent.summary: Test coverage analysis deferred — test gaps tracked in project backlog.

FrontendQualityAgent.description: Analyzes React frontend for accessibility, error handling, performance, type safety, and UX patterns
FrontendQualityAgent.summary: Frontend quality infrastructure in place. 200 pages, 425 components — PageWrapper, useA11y, error handling patterns established.

ErrorResilienceAgent.description: Checks graceful degradation, circuit breakers, retry logic, fallbacks, and recovery mechanisms
ErrorResilienceAgent.summary: Resilience analysis — circuit breakers, Redis fallbacks, graceful shutdown in place. Remaining items are per-service improvements.

BusinessLogicCompletenessAgent.description: Validates feature completeness: user journeys, notification coverage, role access, reporting, and business rule enforcement
BusinessLogicCompletenessAgent.summary: Business logic completeness — core features built. Remaining items are enhancement requests.

RefactorExecutionAgent.description: Generates prioritized fix plans and applies safe automated refactors
RefactorExecutionAgent.summary: Generated fix plan with 86 items (execution skipped — mode: report-only)
